---
name: Adding more personas and roles to the project management processes

title: "Adding more personas and roles to the project management processes"
labels: ["documentation", "process improvement"]
---

## Process document
- `docs/octoacme-roles-and-personas.md`

## Summary of new content
Add several new personas and role definitions to the roles and personas document so that project accountability, handoffs, and role interactions are clearer.

## Why this update is needed
Current documentation covers Developers, Product Managers, and Project Managers, but it does not fully reflect the broader delivery ecosystem needed for cross-functional program management. Adding more personas will reduce ambiguity, improve coordination, and make responsibilities more explicit for release, quality, stakeholder, and architecture activities.

## Suggested content
Potential personas to add:

- **Delivery Lead / Release Manager**
  - Coordinates release planning, readiness checks, deployment activities, and cross-team delivery handoffs.
  - Works closely with Project Managers, Product Managers, QA Leads, and Operations to ensure safe launches.

- **QA / Quality Assurance Lead**
  - Owns quality gates, test strategy, acceptance criteria alignment, and validation planning.
  - Engages with Developers, Product Managers, and Project Managers to confirm requirements are testable and release-ready.

- **Stakeholder Liaison / Business Sponsor**
  - Represents stakeholder interests, supports priority alignment, and helps escalate decisions.
  - Collaborates with Product Managers and Project Managers to keep sponsorship and expectations aligned.

- **Technical Architect / Solution Architect**
  - Guides architecture decisions, resolves technical dependencies, and ensures consistency across solutions.
  - Interacts with Developers and Project Managers to balance technical vision with delivery constraints.

- **Change Manager**
  - Manages change control, approvals, and communication for scope adjustments and release impact.
  - Coordinates with Project Managers, Delivery Leads, and Stakeholder Liaisons on governance and risk.

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Content aligns with existing process docs
- [x] Update improves clarity or closes a documented gap
- [ ] Proposed content has been reviewed with stakeholders (if needed)
